---
title: "How to import/export a database in MySQL/MariaDB"
subtitle: "Note#25"
id: "25"
date: 2022-11-28
date-modified: 2026-03-30
date-format: iso
categories: [notes]
spotlight: ""
---
Use the dump tools to export data, and the client to import it back.

## Export a database

**MySQL:**

```bash
mysqldump -u username -p db_name > backup.sql
```

**MariaDB:**

```bash
mariadb-dump -u username -p db_name > backup.sql
```

### Export specific tables

**MySQL:**

```bash
mysqldump -u username -p db_name table1 table2 > backup.sql
```

**MariaDB:**

```bash
mariadb-dump -u username -p db_name table1 table2 > backup.sql
```

### Export multiple databases

If you want the dump file to include `CREATE DATABASE` and `USE`, add `--databases`:

```bash
mysqldump -u username -p --databases db1 db2 > backup.sql
```

```bash
mariadb-dump -u username -p --databases db1 db2 > backup.sql
```

Use `--databases` when you want the dump file to recreate the original database names. Skip it if you want to restore into a differently named database.

## Import a database

If the dump file does not contain `CREATE DATABASE` / `USE`, create the target database first.

**MySQL:**

```bash
mysql -u username -p db_name < backup.sql
```

**MariaDB:**

```bash
mariadb -u username -p db_name < backup.sql
```

If the dump file already contains `USE db_name`, you can omit the database name:

```bash
mysql -u username -p < backup.sql
```

```bash
mariadb -u username -p < backup.sql
```

If the dump is compressed:

```bash
gunzip < backup.sql.gz | mysql -u username -p db_name
```

```bash
gunzip < backup.sql.gz | mariadb -u username -p db_name
```

## Notes

* `mysqldump` is the standard logical backup tool for MySQL.
* `mariadb-dump` is the current dump tool for MariaDB. `mysqldump` is an older name kept for compatibility in some environments.
* `mariadb-dump` does not include stored procedures or events unless you ask for them with options such as `--routines` and `--events`.
* `mysqlpump` exists in MySQL, but it is deprecated as of MySQL 8.0.34. Do not treat it as the default recommendation.
* For large logical backups, tools such as `mydumper` / `myloader` can be faster because they support parallelism.

## Documentation

* MySQL `mysqldump`: <https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/en/mysqldump.html>
* MySQL `mysql` batch import: <https://dev.mysql.com/doc/refman/en/batch-mode.html>
* MariaDB `mariadb-dump`: <https://mariadb.com/docs/server/clients-and-utilities/backup-restore-and-import-clients/mariadb-dump>
* MariaDB backup and restore overview: <https://mariadb.com/docs/server/server-usage/backup-and-restore/backup-and-restore-overview>
